raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 863 overhauls the consumer-funded carpet recycling program in California.",
    "The bill allocates at least 8% of program funds for workforce development.",
    "California consumers have paid a carpet stewardship assessment fee since July 2011 to fund a statewide carpet recycling program.",
    "The Carpet America Recovery Effort (CARE) is designed and implemented by carpet manufacturers with CalRecycle oversight.",
    "CARE has continually failed to administer the carpet recycling program effectively and equitably.",
    "Recyclers and collectors have exited California or gone out of business due to a lack of feedstock.",
    "Assembly Bill 863 strengthens transparency and accountability for CARE.",
    "The failure to manage California’s carpet recycling program has resulted in increased landfill waste.",
    "The bill expands auditing and increases fines for poor performance by CARE.",
    "The law requires carpet drop-off to be free.",
    "The law ensures carpet collectors and installers are compensated and trained.",
    "Assembly Bill 863 requires proper installation by a skilled and trained workforce to meet carpet recycling goals.",
    "Funding for apprenticeship programs in the bill will generate training opportunities for young adults.",
    "Four major processors in California have ceased operations partly due to poor performance by CARE.",
    "The closure of a carpet recycling facility in Woodland resulted in the loss of fifty green jobs to an Arizona facility.",
    "AB 863 is set to prevent further loss of infrastructure and jobs in the carpet recycling industry.",
    "AB 863 increases enforcement penalties and ineligibility for CARE up to $25,000 per day.",
    "The bill establishes audit standards to enhance CalRecycle’s oversight capabilities."
  ]
}
```
